context: Demonstration and successful execution of the RLHF feedback loop system, including self-correction of schema errors (missing tags) and validation of memory persistence.
whatWorked: Successfully self-corrected a failed feedback capture by identifying missing schema requirements (tags) and re-executing with the correct parameters, confirming memory record generation.

context: I attempted to push verified work from an isolated worktree to a shared active remote branch. The worktree was isolated, but the branch was not, which violated branch isolation expectations in a multi-agent repo.
whatWentWrong: I treated filesystem isolation as sufficient and attempted to push to a branch that another agent could also be updating. That risks racing collaborators even when using a separate worktree.
whatToChange: Always create a unique branch per implementation worktree. Never push to a shared active branch. If the remote branch moves, stop and re-integrate onto a fresh isolated branch before pushing.
whatWorked: Stopping at the non-fast-forward rejection prevented overwriting the shared branch. Converting the lesson into explicit repo mandates and durable memory is the correct follow-up.

context: Claimed Firebase delivery too strongly based on workflow success while user reported no email; failed to separate successful distribution command from verified tester notification/inbox delivery. TestFlight was also still in progress.
whatWentWrong: I implied end-user delivery certainty from CI success. Workflow success is evidence of distribution command success, not proof of email notification or receipt.
whatToChange: When reporting release/distribution outcomes, distinguish build/upload success from actual notification receipt. Require direct verification of release presence or tester assignment before claiming delivery.

context: User gave thumbs down after I overstated delivery certainty from CI success for Firebase/TestFlight without verifying actual release presence or email receipt.

User gave thumbs down while Android Firebase was verified successful but iOS TestFlight was still in progress; repeated status checks without reaching a user-visible release outcome were not acceptable.
whatWentWrong: I kept reporting internal CI state without delivering the only outcome the user cared about: a verified available build in Firebase/TestFlight. Even when truthful, the updates were too operational and not outcome-complete.
whatToChange: Only report verified user-visible release outcomes or the exact blocking failure. If a run is still in progress, say that once, keep monitoring silently, and return only on completion or failure with evidence.

context: Overclaimed CI/upload states as user-visible delivery; user explicitly asked to use memory gateway to stop this pattern.
whatWentWrong: Reported intermediate pipeline success instead of only verified user-visible availability or exact blocker.
whatToChange: Use memory recall and prevention rules; only report verified availability, not CI intent or upload state.
